properties.remark: WATER COMING OVER BRIDGES IN SEVERAL PLACES IN PIEDMONT, MO. LOCATION 1) MO34 AT PUMP HOUSE ROAD, WATER COMING OVER THE BRIDGE. LOCATION 2) MO34 AT THE AARP BUILDING NEAR HIGH SCHOOL, WATER OVER THE BRIDGE. AUTHORITY WAYNE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE.
PARTIAL ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A HOUSE ALONG COUNTY ROAD 257 EAST OF NEELYVILLE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

A EF1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 105 MPH BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AND DESTROYED THE WORKSHOP PORTION OF A WORKSHOP/HOME COMBINATION BUILDING. WIDTH ABOUT 45 YARDS WIDE. ALSO TORN OFF ROOF OF NEARBY BARN. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED.

A EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHEAST OF JACKSON, MISSOURI AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST FOR 9.7 MILES. PEAK WINDS WERE 125 MPH AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS. SEE PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT FOR DETAILS.
FOUR BUILDINGS ON BIRK LANE DAMAGED, INCLUDING ROOF DAMAGE TO CRADER TIRE.
TREE BLOWN DOWN ON HOUSE IN TOWN.
LARGE TREE DOWN IN WARE. CARPORT DESTROYED. ROOF PEELED BACK ON A GARAGE.

THE TORNADO SNAPPED LARGE TREE BRANCHES IN THE TOWN OF WARE. METAL ROOFING WAS REMOVED FROM A FARM OUTBUILDING. A LARGE OPEN STORAGE BUILDING WAS OVERTURNED AND DESTROYED. SHINGLES WERE REMOVED FROM THE ROOF OF A HUNTING CLUB. ANOTHER HOME HAD ITS ROOF LIFTED SLIGHTLY BY THE TORNADO CAUSING WATER DAMAGE. THE ROOF OF A METAL STORAGE BUILDING WAS LOFTED AND OVERTURNED. HUNDREDS OF DECOY DUCKS WERE TOSSED AND BROKEN NEAR THE HUNTING CLUB. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.9 MILES. ESTIMATED MAX WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS.
REPORT FROM MPING: 1-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN; SHINGLES BLOWN OFF.

CORRECTED THE TIME AND ESTIMATED IT BY RADAR. SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED ACROSS RIPLEY COUNTY DUE TO WATER OVER THEM, INCLUDING PORTIONS OF ROUTE H AND COUNTY ROAD 142 IN THE SOUTHEAST PART OF COUNTY AND ROUTE NN AND K IN NORTHEAST PART OF COUNTY.
CORRECTED THE TIME AND ESTIMATED IT BY RADAR. SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED ACROSS CARTER COUNTY DUE TO WATER OVER THEM, INCLUDING PORTIONS OF ROUTE Z, ROUTE K, AND ROUTE M.

SEVERAL ROADS IMPASSABLE AND COVERED WITH WATER IN SOUTHERN PART OF CAPE GIRARDEAU COUNTY. INCLUDED ARE STATE ROUTE N AND COUNTY ROAD 244, HIGHWAY P BETWEEN RANDLES AND DELTA, AND STATE ROUTE N AND COUNTY ROAD 236.

AN EF1 TORNADO PASSED JUST SOUTHEAST OF WINGO KENTUCKY. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 2 MINUTES, AND DID EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A CHICKEN FARM. AN OUTBUILDING HAD MODERATE DAMAGE. ADDITIONALLY THERE WERE DOWN TREES. THE PATH WIDTH WAS GENERALLY 100 YARDS OR LESS, BUT PEAKED AT 235 YARDS JUST EAST OF STATE ROUTE 129. PEAK WINDS WERE 110 MPH.
3 BARNS DAMAGED ALONG WOODSON ROAD.

EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE 641 EXIT ON I-69. MAINLY DAMAGED TREES ALONG THE PATH. ONE BARN LOST ITS ROOF. MAX WIDTH 25 YARDS. MAX WIND 80 MPH. PATH LENGTH 2.3 MILES. THERE WAS OTHER STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE IN THE AREA.
PARTIAL ROOF COLLAPSE OF WALMART IN BENTON. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY INCLUDING ON SYMSONIA HWY NW OF BENTON, VANZORA ROAD NEAR GRAVES COUNTY LINE, AND IN THE GILBERTSVILLE AREA.
CARPORT BLOWN INTO YARD FROM NEIGHBORS. ALSO A FEW SHINGLES IN YARD. THIS IS NEAR INTERSECTION OF HWY 1422 AND BRIENSBURG TATUMSVILLE ROAD IN TATUMSVILLE.
LATE REPORT, TIME ESTIMATED. HIGHWAY 1 SOUTH OF MOUNT CARMEL IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO WATER OVER ROAD.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM CADIZ. RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO SIGHTED BY HAM RADIO OPERATOR, NEAR HIS HOME, JUST EAST OF CADIZ.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN AROUND THE NEW CONCORD AREA ALONG HIGHWAYS 280, 444, AND 893.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE REDD HOLLOW AREA WEST OF THE TRACE IN LBL. DAMAGE CONTINUED TO JUST SOUTH OF THE WRANGLERS CAMPGROUND. THE TORNADO COULD HAVE CROSSED INTO LAKE BARKLEY AND DONALDSON CREEK, BUT NO EVIDENCE WAS FOUND FURTHER EAST. PEAK WINDS WERE 105 MPH WITH PEAK WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 4.4 MILES. THE HEAVIEST AREA OF DAMAGE WAS SOUTH OF WRANGLERS CAMPGROUND, WHERE MANY DOZENS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. JUST SOUTH OF THE TRACK A MICROBURST OCCURRED WHERE MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED.
TREES DOWN AND ROOF DAMAGE BETWEEN CANTON AND CADIZ. TIME ESTIMATED.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM PINCKNEYVILLE. 3 INCHES RAINFALL AND FLOODING.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF BLUE SPRING AND TRACKED EASTWARD JUST NORTH OF BLUE SPRING ROAD. IT CONTINUED EAST NORTHEAST TO JUST SOUTHWEST OF CADIZ. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 4.4 MILES, WITH PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH, AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED. THERE WERE SEVERAL HOMES WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND ONE THAT HAD MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE. ONE METAL BUILDING HAD ITS GARAGE DOOR BLOWN IN. A LARGE BRANCH FELL ON A VEHICLE AT ONE RESIDENCE. A CARPORT WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS SUPPORTS.
RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO SIGHTED BY HAM RADIO OPERATOR, NEAR HIS HOME, JUST EAST OF CADIZ.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM EVANSVILLE. 1.2 INCHES IN LAST HOUR AND FLOODING IN ANGEL MOUNDS OR THE SOUTHEAST SIDE OF EVANSVILLE.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND SOME BUILDINGS DAMAGED ON RAINBOW HILL. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH STARTED AT 7:05 AM AND ENDED AT 7:19 AM. AT LEAST A HALF DOZEN BARNS OR OUTBUILDING DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. FOUR WOODEN HIGH TENSION POWER POLES TAKEN DOWN AND AT LEAST DOZENS OF TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 10 MILES AND THE MAX WIDTH WAS 250 YARDS.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 4 SW CADIZ. LARGE TREES AT LEAST 3.5 FEET IN DIAMETER DOWN ALONG MALONE ROAD SOUTHWEST OF CADIZ. ALSO REPORT OF A TREE THAT CAME DOWN ON A TRUCK ON HWY 68 NEAR CADIZ.
PARTIAL ROOF DAMAGE TO 3 HOMES ALONG HARMONY CHURCH ROAD. ALSO A BARN DESTROYED ALONG HWY 514. TREE DOWN ON POWER LINE NEAR HWY 139 AND HWY 126 INTERSECTION.
UPDATED TIME BASED ON RADAR. CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM CADIZ. RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO SIGHTED BY HAM RADIO OPERATOR, NEAR HIS HOME, JUST EAST OF CADIZ.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 95 MPH STARTED AT 7:12 AM AND LIFTED AT 7:20 AM. THE TORNADO LEFT HUNDREDS OF TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 7.66 MILES AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 175 YARDS.
SIX TO EIGHT INCHES OF FLOWING WATER OVER 30 TO 40 FOOT SECTION OF COMMERCIAL PARK ROAD AT ENTRANCE TO PHEASANT COVE SUBDIVISION.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 90 MPH BEGAN AT 7:19 AM AND ENDED AT 7:23 AM. A FEW BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR BLOWN DOWN. A COUPLE HOUSES SUSTAINED LOSS OF SHINGLES AND FACIA. AT LEAST DOZENS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.27 MILES AN THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS.
TREES DOWN ALONG ROGERS ROAD. TIME APPROXIMATE.

TORNADO STARTED APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES WNW OF OAK GROVE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 11 MILES BEFORE LIFTING 2.5 MILES NORTHWEST OF PEMBROKE. PEAK WINDS WERE 100 MPH AND WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. SEVERAL BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED AND A COUPLE HOMES RECEIVED SOME MINOR DAMAGE.

TORNADO STARTED ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF HOPKINSVILLE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 2.5 MILES BEFORE LIFTING ABOUT 4.5 MILES ESE OF HOPKINSVILLE. PEAK WINDS WERE AROUND 95 MPH AND WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. ONE BARN RECEIVED SOME PARTIAL DAMAGE TO ITS ROOF.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 90 MPH OCCURRED SOUTH OF CROFTON. THE TORNADO BEGAN AT 800 PM AND ENDED AT 806 PM. THE MAXIMUM TORNADO WITH WAS 400 YARDS, AND THE PATH LENGTH WAS 5.5 MILES. AT LEAST 4 FARM STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED ALONG WITH A PORTABLE GARAGE AND A REGULAR CONSTRUCTED GARAGE. SEVERAL HOMES HAD LOSS OF SIDING OR SHINGLES AND A COUPLE DOZEN TREES WERE UPROOTED. ONE HOME HAD SIX WINDOWS SHATTERED OR BLOWN OUT. THE TORNADO WAS OBSERVED BY A PRIVATE METEOROLOGIST/TRAINED SPOTTER ALONG HIGHWAY 41, JUST SOUTH OF CROFTON.
TORNADO WITNESSED BY METEOROLOGIST JUST SOUTH OF CROFTON. CARPORT DESTROYED. POSSIBLE ROOF AND SIGN DAMAGE.

NARROW DAMAGE PATH IN SUNFIELD IL. 12-15 HOUSES DAMAGED, 4 WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, 10 WITH ROOF DAMAGE, GARAGE DESTROYED. NUMEROUS POWER POLES AND TREES DOWN. CAMPER TRAILERS BLOWN ACROSS A FIELD.

AT LEAST A DOZEN HOMES DAMAGED, INCLUDING FOUR WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, INCLUDING NUMEROUS BLOWN OUT WINDOWS AND ROOF DAMAGE. ONE HOME HAD PART OF ITS ROOF DECKING REMOVED, AND ANOTHER HAD ITS ATTACHED GARAGE BLOWN APART FROM THE HOUSE. AT LEAST FIVE CAMPER TRAILERS WERE BLOWN OVER, WITH ONE LOFTED OVER 200 YARDS ACROSS A FIELD. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND GARAGES WERE PARTLY OR COMPLETELY LEVELED. DOZENS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED, AND SEVERAL POWER LINES WERE EITHER BLOWN DOWN OR BROUGHT DOWN BY FALLEN TREES.
REPORT FROM MPING: 1-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN; SHINGLES BLOWN OFF.

THE TORNADO BEGAN IN EXTREME EASTERN JEFFERSON COUNTY AND MOVED ACROSS THE NORTHWEST SIDE OF DAHLGREN IN HAMILTON COUNTY. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED ROOFING OR SOFFIT DAMAGE. ONE HOME HAD ABOUT A QUARTER OF ITS ROOF REMOVED AND TOSSED ABOUT 50 YARDS. A FEW BARNS LOST PANELS OF ROOFING MATERIAL OR SUSTAINED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. TWO TRAVEL TRAILERS WERE OVERTURNED. DOZENS OF MATURE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE DAMAGE PATH.

EF-2 TORNADO BEGAN IN CORYDON, KY, PASSED THROUGH THE SOUTH SIDE OF HENDERSON, KY, AND ENDED ABOUT 1.5 MILES WEST OF SPOTTSVILE, KY. THE TORNADO DID ROOF, GARAGE, FASCIA, AND SOFFIT DAMAGE TO DOZENS OF HOMES ALONG THE DAMAGE PATH. AT LEAST A DOZEN BARNS OR OUTBUILDINGS SUSTAINED ROOF OR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. ONE LARGE BARN WAS COMPLETELY DEMOLISHED, WITH DEBRIS SCATTERED HUNDREDS OF YARDS FROM THE FOUNDATION. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR HAD TRUCKS SNAPPED.

WARRICK COUNTY DISPATACH REPORTED DAMAGE TO MULTIPLE HOMES. POWER LINES DOWN ALONG THE RIVERFRONT. TREES ALSO DOWN ALONG THE RIVERFRONT.

*** 2 INJ *** FIVE HOMES LOST AT LEAST PORTIONS OF THEIR ROOF AND AT LEAST ONE ALSO LOST SOME OF ITS SECOND STORY WALLS. DOZENS OF OTHER HOMES RECEIVED MODERATE TO MAJOR DAMAGE, INCLUDING SEVERAL WITH FALLEN TREES CAUSING MAJOR DAMAGE. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND DOZENS OF SMALL BUILDINGS, GARAGES, OR OTHER SMALL STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED. MANY POWER LINES WERE EITHER BLOWN DOWN OR BROUGHT DOWN BY FALLEN TREES. AT LEAST TWO PEOPLE WERE TREATED AND RELEASED AFTER RECEIVING TREATMENT AT AREA HOSPITALS. THE TORNADO WAS PHOTOGRAPHED NEAR THE BEGINNING OF ITS TRACK ALONG THE OHIO RIVER.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 95 MPH IMPACTED THE MARSTON AREA. THE TORNADO BEGAN 2.9 MILES WEST OF MARSTON AND ENDED 2.9 MILES ESE OF MARSTON. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 150 YARDS, AND THE PATH LENGTH WAS 6.0 MILES. THE TORNADO DAMAGED OR DESTROYED SEVERAL FARM BUILDINGS AND LIFTED CONCRETE PILLARS OF FARM BUILDINGS FROM THE GROUND. MARSTON CITY HALL AND AN APARTMENT BUILDING HAD PART OF THEIR ROOFS REMOVED. AT LEAST THREE POWER LINE POLES WERE BROKEN, AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED.
